description: Automatic game image mounter - Mount .ffpkg, .exfat, and .ffpfs images automatically without manual intervention

changelog: Fixed issues with mounting in /system_ex/app
Experimental support for .ffpfsc images has been added. Scan depth can be left at `scan_depth=1`.
**Do not use with MicroMount!**
fix: PFS mount issue
fix2: removed forced unmounting of nullfs due to a conflict with kstuff, causing KP
Critical fix for memory allocation issue in multi-image scenarios
Improved error handling for corrupted image headers
Fixed race condition in mount/unmount operations
Enhanced compatibility with newer SDK versions
Performance optimizations for image detection
⚠ This is a pre-release version. Use with caution.
Experimental support for dynamic image switching
Testing improved notification system
WIP: Background service optimization
Added support for nested directory structures in image paths
Improved auto-detection of image types
Fixed unmounting issues when switching between games
Enhanced logging for troubleshooting
Better resource cleanup on exit
Fixed compatibility issues with FW 5.50
Improved stability during game launch sequences
Better handling of large image files (>50GB)
Optimized memory usage for better system performance
Fixed issue with certain exFAT formatted images
Enhanced multi-image support for games with DLC
Improved notification messages
Fixed bug preventing auto-mount on USB drives
Added configuration file support for custom mount paths
Better error messages for failed mount operations
Added support for .ffpfs image format
Improved scanning speed for large directories
Fixed memory leak in background monitoring service
Better integration with system mount points
Reduced CPU usage during idle periods
The first beta of the 1.6, which will receive new features.
Instant game mounting has been restored, eliminating the need to wait for Kstuff.
To avoid kernel panics, load Kstuff/EthaHEN last in autoload.txt.
Added support backports for games in images.
Place the unpacked backport in /data/backports/TITLEID and load backpork.elf.
Added "force_mount" option
A known issue: when using ffpkg, the console won't shut down or restart on older firmware versions. The recommended solution is to use exfat. I'll try to fix this in the next release.
Fixed issues with remounting unmounted images (this happens when the first /dev/lvd* numbers remain occupied and the system frees them, unmounting all mounted images).
Changed the mount folder to /data/imgmnt to align with Dump Installer. The old /data/ufsmnt folder must be manually deleted.
You can use config.ini to change the R/W mode individually for each image, as some games require the ability to write to the file system.
Improved verbosity of mounting error messages.
Added caching of app.db requests.
fix1 - Added a forced mount option for damaged file systems. It's better to fix the errors than to use this option.
Added automatic unmount on game exit
Improved compatibility with FW 5.xx
Fixed path handling for images with special characters
Better handling of fragmented image files
Performance improvements for large image libraries
Enhanced error detection and reporting
Added support for image verification before mounting
Fixed timeout issues with slow USB devices
Improved mount point selection algorithm
Better cleanup of stale mount points
Optimized mounting parameters and the speed has been increased.
Automatic param.json correction
Extended error diagnostics output
Optional recursive folder scanning (slower)
Automatic disk image size calculation in creation scripts
Fixed exFAT image creation on Windows
Expanded documentation, examples, and troubleshooting guidance
fix: Fix source stability checks to avoid false wait/skip loops
fix: exFAT image builds in Windows
Please read README.md
Completely refactored
Working with a large number of folders

Correct game registration
Mounting images as read-only by default
Correct unmounting and deleting folders on shutdown/restart
The ability to customize folders for scanning
Complete rewrite with modular architecture
Added plugin system for extensibility
Improved performance and stability
Better integration with system services
Enhanced image format detection
Fixed regression in exFAT mounting from 1.4+test
Improved stability on FW 3.xx
Better handling of mount failures
Enhanced debug logging
